document: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 of the European Parliament and of the Council (the EU AI Act), Official Journal text. Injected as both PDF (sources/regulation/eu_ai_act_2024_1689.pdf, 144 pages) and a normalized text extract (sources/regulation/eu_ai_act_2024_1689.txt).
note: Questions target specific penalty amounts, phased application dates, and risk-tier rules that must be looked up in the injected text. The model cannot answer these reliably from memory. Answers are graded against the injected regulation text.

questions_and_answers.question: What is the maximum administrative fine for infringing the prohibited-AI-practices rules (Article 5), expressed both as a fixed amount and as a percentage of worldwide annual turnover?
questions_and_answers.answer: Up to EUR 35 000 000, or, if the offender is an undertaking, up to 7% of its total worldwide annual turnover for the preceding financial year, whichever is higher.
questions_and_answers.anchor: Art. 99: 'non-compliance ... referred to in Article 5 shall be subject to administrative fines of up to EUR 35 000 000 or ... up to 7% of its total worldwide annual turnover'

questions_and_answers.question: What is the maximum fine for supplying incorrect, incomplete or misleading information to notified bodies or competent authorities in reply to a request?
questions_and_answers.answer: Up to EUR 7 500 000, or, if the offender is an undertaking, up to 1% of its total worldwide annual turnover, whichever is higher.
questions_and_answers.anchor: Art. 99: 'supply of incorrect ... information ... administrative fines of up to EUR 7 500 000 or ... up to 1% of its total worldwide annual turnover'

questions_and_answers.question: There is an intermediate penalty tier for non-compliance with provisions other than Article 5 (e.g. obligations on operators or notified bodies). What is its fixed-amount cap and turnover percentage?
questions_and_answers.answer: Up to EUR 15 000 000, or, if the offender is an undertaking, up to 3% of its total worldwide annual turnover, whichever is higher.
questions_and_answers.anchor: Art. 99: 'EUR 15 000 000 or ... up to 3% of its total worldwide annual turnover'

questions_and_answers.question: From what date does the Regulation generally apply, and on what date did it enter into force relative to that?
questions_and_answers.answer: The Regulation applies from 2 August 2026 in general. (It entered into force earlier - 20 days after publication in the Official Journal, i.e. 1 August 2024 - with application phased afterward.)
questions_and_answers.anchor: Final article: 'It shall apply from 2 August 2026.'

questions_and_answers.question: The application is phased. By when do (a) the prohibitions and general provisions (Chapters I and II) apply, and (b) the rules on general-purpose AI models, governance and penalties (the 2 August 2025 tranche) apply?
questions_and_answers.answer: (a) Chapters I and II - including the prohibited practices - apply from 2 February 2025. (b) The GPAI-model rules, the governance/notifying-authority structure, and the penalties provisions apply from 2 August 2025. Certain high-risk obligations apply from 2 August 2027.
questions_and_answers.anchor: Final article: 'Chapters I and II shall apply from 2 February 2025; (b) ... shall apply from 2 August 2025 ... shall apply from 2 August 2027'

questions_and_answers.question: Where in the Regulation is the list of high-risk AI system use-cases found (the annex), and name the high-level risk categories the Act uses to classify AI systems.
questions_and_answers.answer: The high-risk use-case list is in Annex III. The Act classifies AI by risk: prohibited (unacceptable risk, Article 5), high-risk (Annex III / Article 6), and lower-risk systems subject to transparency obligations; general-purpose AI models are governed separately.
questions_and_answers.anchor: Annex III lists high-risk use cases; Article 5 prohibited practices; Article 6 high-risk classification

scoring_notes: Each answer must come from the injected regulation text. Reward exact penalty amounts (35M/15M/7.5M and 7%/3%/1%) and the exact phased dates (2 Feb 2025, 2 Aug 2025, 2 Aug 2026, 2 Aug 2027) with article/annex citations. Penalize wrong amounts, swapped tiers, wrong dates, or answers given generically without reading the text.
dynamic_content_note: The injected OJ text is frozen and authoritative for this task. Judge answers against the injected PDF/text.
